title: What Does My Guilt About This Say About My Worthiness Story?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for unpacking the guilt that comes with unearned money
excerpt: A journal prompt set for examining the guilt that arrives with an inheritance, a gift, or a windfall. Works through what the guilt is protecting, where the earning rule came from, and what keeping the money without guilt would actually mean.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've received money you didn't earn and guilt, discomfort, or the urge to give it away is running alongside the money.

content.text: A journal set for the guilt that arrives with money that wasn't earned. Inheritance, a gift from someone who had more, a legal settlement, a win — the common thread is that the usual transaction (effort for payment) didn't happen. These prompts work through what the guilt is doing and where it came from.
What is my relationship with this money right now?
Describe in plain terms. Is it in your account? Are you avoiding looking at it? Have you already begun to spend it away?
What feeling comes up most when I think about keeping it?
Name the feeling without explaining it. Guilt, discomfort, relief, grief — write whichever is there.
Where did I learn that money only counts if I earned it?
Was there a person who said this? A household rule? Something you absorbed without being told?
What does the guilt say I should do with this money?
Write the instruction the guilt is giving you. Give it away, spend it quickly, donate most of it, hold it loosely. What does it want you to do?
If this money were an amount I had earned, what would I do with it?
Notice whether the answer is different from what you're actually doing. The gap is the belief in action.
